zh.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford是Robert de Tosny de Stafford和Adaleza的兒子，於大約1075年出生在英國斯塔福爾史東。他於1100年與Maud Moolte結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾被列為high sheriff of staffordshire。他去世於大約1138年在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德享年65歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford was born about 1075, in Stone, Staffordshire, England as the son of Robert de Tosny de Stafford and Adaleza. He married Maud Moolte in 1100. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as high sheriff of staffordshire in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. He died about 1138, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Stafford, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford est né environ 1075, à Stone, Staffordshire, Angleterre, fils de Robert de Tosny de Stafford et Adaleza. Il a épousé Maud Moolte en 1100. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of staffordshire à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé environ 1138, à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford wurde um 1075 in Stone, Staffordshire, England, als Sohn von Robert de Tosny de Stafford und Adaleza geboren. Er hat 1100 Maud Moolte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als high sheriff of staffordshire in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb um 1138 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Stafford, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Stafford, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford è nato il circa 1075 nel Stone, Staffordshire, England come figlio di Robert de Tosny de Stafford e Adaleza. Lui sposato Maud Moolte nel 1100. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of staffordshire nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì circa 1138, nel Stafford, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Stafford, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Staffordは約1075年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアストーンでRobert de Tosny de StaffordとAdalezaの息子として生まれた。1100年にMaud Moolteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでの職業はhigh sheriff of staffordshireと記録されている。彼は約1138年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードで65歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford(은)는 대략 1075년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스톤에서 Robert de Tosny de Stafford(와)과 Adaleza의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1100년 Maud Moolte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 high sheriff of staffordshire이었다. 그는 대략 1138년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford nasceu aproximadamente 1075, em Stone, Staffordshire, Inglaterra filho de Robert de Tosny de Stafford e Adaleza. Ele casou-se com Maud Moolte em 1100. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of staffordshire em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1138, em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford родился около 1075 г. в местности стоун, стаффордшир, Англия. Его родителей звали Robert de Tosny de Stafford и Adaleza. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Moolte в 1100 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии high sheriff of staffordshire в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер около 1138 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия.
